rendered_prompt:
You are repairing an existing DALi dali-ui Markdown guide.
Return only the complete repaired Markdown document.
Do not rewrite the guide. Only edit the listed issue spans and the smallest surrounding context needed.
Preserve unrelated headings, ordering, and correct content as-is.
Keep the repaired user-facing guide in en only.
Remove Korean, Japanese, Chinese, or other non-English prose when it appears in the guide.
Do not invent APIs. Use only provided allowed symbols, property owners, usage patterns, or local helpers defined in a code block.

Source-grounded repair requirements:
- Repair only the problematic spans and the smallest surrounding context needed; do not rewrite the whole document.
- For each listed issue, use the repository evidence and context pack to verify the corrected wording or code before returning.
- If an issue reveals the same API misuse nearby, fix the nearby equivalent misuse too; do not make unrelated style rewrites.
- After repair, re-check the edited lines against public headers, implementation files, samples, or UTCs when needed.
- For every edited C++ code block, inline API statement, and backticked API name, verify:
  - receiver method exists on the receiver type
  - static methods are called statically and member methods are called on valid instances
  - assignment target is compatible with the method return type
  - argument count, argument type, and overload shape match the public API
  - enum owner and enum value both exist
  - property constants use the correct owner and value type
  - signal callback signatures match the public signal typedef
  - code blocks do not rely on inferred namespaces or overloads from naming alone

Original Markdown:
---
title: Animated Image View
sidebar_label: Animated Image View
category: views-components
---

# Animated Image View

`Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView` displays animated image resources such as GIF or WebP files, or frame image sequences, and gives dali-ui apps typed control over source selection, playback, loading, and masking.

## Table of Contents

- [Create an Animated Image View](#create-an-animated-image-view)
- [Play, Pause, Stop, and Seek Frames](#play-pause-stop-and-seek-frames)
- [Use Frame Image Sequences](#use-frame-image-sequences)
- [Tune Playback and Looping](#tune-playback-and-looping)
- [Handle Loading and Completion Signals](#handle-loading-and-completion-signals)
- [Configure Sizing, Sampling, and Color](#configure-sizing-sampling-and-color)
- [Use Loading Images, Pixel Areas, and Alpha Masks](#use-loading-images-pixel-areas-and-alpha-masks)
- [Use Property Constants When Integrating with Property Systems](#use-property-constants-when-integrating-with-property-systems)

## Create an Animated Image View

Create the view with `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New`. Pass a resource URL when the animated file is known up front, or create an empty view and set the source later with `SetResourceUrl`.

`Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView` is a `Dali::Ui::View`, so keep it in the dali-ui view tree and use the typed `AnimatedImageView` APIs for image behavior.

```cpp
#include <dali-ui-foundation/public-api/animated-image-view.h>

Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView CreateLoadingAnimation()
{
  return Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/loading.gif")
    .SetLoopCount(-1)
    .SetFrameSpeedFactor(1.0f)
    .SetFrameDelay(500);
}
```

To create the view first and assign a resource later:

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New();

image
  .SetResourceUrl("images/activity.webp")
  .SetLoopCount(3)
  .Play();
```

Use `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::DownCast` when code receives a generic handle and needs animated-image-specific controls.

```cpp
void ConfigureIfAnimated(Dali::BaseHandle handle)
{
  Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::DownCast(handle);

  if(image)
  {
    image.SetLoopCount(-1).Play();
  }
}
```

## Play, Pause, Stop, and Seek Frames

Use `Play`, `Pause`, and `Stop` for playback control. Use `JumpToFrame` to move to a specific frame index. Query the current state with `GetPlayState`, `GetCurrentFrame`, and `GetTotalFrame`.

```cpp
void StartAnimation(Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image)
{
  image.Play();

  if(image.GetPlayState() == Dali::Ui::AnimatedImage::PlayState::PLAYING)
  {
    const int currentFrame = image.GetCurrentFrame();
    const int totalFrames  = image.GetTotalFrame();

    (void)currentFrame;
    (void)totalFrames;
  }
}

void PauseAtPreviewFrame(Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image)
{
  image
    .Pause()
    .JumpToFrame(5);
}

void StopAnimation(Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image)
{
  image.Stop();
}
```

`SetStopBehavior` controls which frame remains visible when playback stops. Use `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImage::StopBehavior::CURRENT_FRAME`, `FIRST_FRAME`, or `LAST_FRAME`.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/progress.gif");

image
  .SetStopBehavior(Dali::Ui::AnimatedImage::StopBehavior::LAST_FRAME)
  .Play();
```

## Use Frame Image Sequences

`SetResourceUrls` accepts a `Dali::Vector<Dali::String>` and plays the listed images as animation frames. This is useful when frames are shipped as separate PNG or WebP files. Setting a URL array overrides any single URL source for playback.

```cpp
Dali::Vector<Dali::String> frames;
frames.PushBack(Dali::String("images/spinner-001.png"));
frames.PushBack(Dali::String("images/spinner-002.png"));
frames.PushBack(Dali::String("images/spinner-003.png"));
frames.PushBack(Dali::String("images/spinner-004.png"));

Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New();

image
  .SetResourceUrls(frames)
  .SetBatchSize(4)
  .SetCacheSize(8)
  .SetFrameDelay(100)
  .SetLoopCount(-1)
  .Play();
```

Use `GetResourceUrls` to inspect the current frame list, and `GetResourceUrl` when the view is configured from a single animated image file.

```cpp
const Dali::Vector<Dali::String>& currentFrames = image.GetResourceUrls();
Dali::String currentFile = image.GetResourceUrl();

(void)currentFrames;
(void)currentFile;
```

To switch back from a frame list to a single animated file, clear the URL vector before assigning the file URL.

```cpp
Dali::Vector<Dali::String> noFrames;

image
  .SetResourceUrls(noFrames)
  .SetResourceUrl("images/complete.gif")
  .Play();
```

## Tune Playback and Looping

`SetLoopCount` controls repeat behavior. Use `-1` for continuous looping, `0` to prevent playback, or a positive value for an exact loop count.

`SetFrameSpeedFactor` changes playback speed. Values between `0.0f` and `1.0f` slow the animation, and values above `1.0f` speed it up.

`SetFrameDelay` sets a frame delay override in milliseconds. Use `-1` to use the frame delay embedded in the image file.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/status.gif");

image
  .SetLoopCount(3)
  .SetFrameSpeedFactor(2.0f)
  .SetFrameDelay(200)
  .Play();

const int loopCount       = image.GetLoopCount();
const float speedFactor   = image.GetFrameSpeedFactor();
const int frameDelayMsecs = image.GetFrameDelay();

(void)loopCount;
(void)speedFactor;
(void)frameDelayMsecs;
```

For frame-sequence animations, combine `SetBatchSize` and `SetCacheSize` with `SetFrameDelay` to control how many frames are pre-loaded in each batch and kept in cache.

```cpp
image
  .SetBatchSize(4)
  .SetCacheSize(12)
  .SetFrameDelay(80);
```

## Handle Loading and Completion Signals

Use `ResourceReadySignal` to run code when the image resource is ready for display. Use `AnimationFinishedSignal` when finite playback completes all configured loops.

Both signals use `Dali::Ui::View` as the callback argument.

```cpp
class AnimationController : public Dali::ConnectionTracker
{
public:
  void Setup()
  {
    mImage = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/intro.gif");

    mImage.ResourceReadySignal().Connect(this, &AnimationController::OnResourceReady);
    mImage.AnimationFinishedSignal().Connect(this, &AnimationController::OnAnimationFinished);

    mImage
      .SetLoopCount(1)
      .Play();
  }

private:
  void OnResourceReady(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::DownCast(view);

    if(image)
    {
      const int totalFrames = image.GetTotalFrame();
      (void)totalFrames;
    }
  }

  void OnAnimationFinished(Dali::Ui::View view)
  {
    Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::DownCast(view);

    if(image)
    {
      image.Stop();
    }
  }

private:
  Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView mImage;
};
```

`GetLoadingStatus` returns the current visual resource status for the image.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::Visual::ResourceStatus status = image.GetLoadingStatus();
(void)status;
```

## Configure Sizing, Sampling, and Color

Use desired width and height as decoding hints when the app knows the target image size. Use fitting and sampling modes to control how frames are scaled in the view.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/banner.gif");

image
  .SetDesiredWidth(320)
  .SetDesiredHeight(180)
  .SetImageLoadWithViewSize(true)
  .SetFittingMode(Dali::Ui::Image::FittingMode::FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO)
  .SetSamplingMode(Dali::Ui::Image::SamplingMode::BOX_THEN_LINEAR);
```

The matching getters let app code read back the active configuration.

```cpp
const int desiredWidth  = image.GetDesiredWidth();
const int desiredHeight = image.GetDesiredHeight();
const bool viewSized    = image.IsImageLoadWithViewSizeEnabled();

Dali::Ui::Image::FittingMode fittingMode = image.GetFittingMode();
Dali::Ui::Image::SamplingMode samplingMode = image.GetSamplingMode();

(void)desiredWidth;
(void)desiredHeight;
(void)viewSized;
(void)fittingMode;
(void)samplingMode;
```

Use `SetImageColor` to apply a color to the image and `SetPreMultipliedAlpha` when the resource uses pre-multiplied alpha.

```cpp
image
  .SetImageColor(Dali::Ui::UiColor(1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f, 1.0f))
  .SetPreMultipliedAlpha(true);

Dali::Ui::UiColor color = image.GetImageColor();
const bool preMultiplied = image.IsPreMultipliedAlpha();

(void)color;
(void)preMultiplied;
```

## Use Loading Images, Pixel Areas, and Alpha Masks

`SetPlaceholderUrl` assigns an image shown while the animated resource is loading. This is useful for remote or large resources.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New();

image
  .SetPlaceholderUrl("images/loading-preview.png")
  .SetResourceUrl("images/remote-preview.gif")
  .SetLoopCount(-1)
  .Play();

Dali::String loadingImage = image.GetPlaceholderUrl();
(void)loadingImage;
```

Use `SetPixelArea` to display a normalized sub-region of the animated image. The value is `(x, y, width, height)` in normalized coordinates.

```cpp
image.SetPixelArea(Dali::Vector4(0.0f, 0.0f, 0.5f, 0.5f));

Dali::Vector4 area = image.GetPixelArea();
(void)area;
```

Use `SetAlphaMaskUrl` with `SetMaskingMode` to apply an alpha mask. `SetCropToMask` controls whether the visible image is cropped to the mask bounds.

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView maskedImage =
  Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/avatar.gif");

maskedImage
  .SetAlphaMaskUrl("images/circle-mask.png")
  .SetMaskingMode(Dali::Ui::Image::MaskingType::MASKING_ON_RENDERING)
  .SetCropToMask(true)
  .SetFittingMode(Dali::Ui::Image::FittingMode::FIT_KEEP_ASPECT_RATIO)
  .Play();

Dali::String maskUrl = maskedImage.GetAlphaMaskUrl();
const bool cropToMask = maskedImage.IsCropToMask();
Dali::Ui::Image::MaskingType maskingMode = maskedImage.GetMaskingMode();

(void)maskUrl;
(void)cropToMask;
(void)maskingMode;
```

## Use Property Constants When Integrating with Property Systems

Application code should prefer typed APIs such as `SetResourceUrl`, `SetLoopCount`, `SetFrameDelay`, and `SetAlphaMaskUrl`. The `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property` constants are the property owner surface for integrations that need property indices.

`Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property` owns these animated-image properties:

| Property constant | Typed API to prefer in application code |
| --- | --- |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::IMAGE` | `SetResourceUrl`, `GetResourceUrl` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::IMAGE_URLS` | `SetResourceUrls`, `GetResourceUrls` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::LOOP_COUNT` | `SetLoopCount`, `GetLoopCount` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::STOP_BEHAVIOR` | `SetStopBehavior`, `GetStopBehavior` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::FRAME_SPEED_FACTOR` | `SetFrameSpeedFactor`, `GetFrameSpeedFactor` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::BATCH_SIZE` | `SetBatchSize`, `GetBatchSize` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::CACHE_SIZE` | `SetCacheSize`, `GetCacheSize` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::FRAME_DELAY` | `SetFrameDelay`, `GetFrameDelay` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::IMAGE_COLOR` | `SetImageColor`, `GetImageColor` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::PRE_MULTIPLIED_ALPHA` | `SetPreMultipliedAlpha`, `IsPreMultipliedAlpha` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::FITTING_MODE` | `SetFittingMode`, `GetFittingMode` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::SAMPLING_MODE` | `SetSamplingMode`, `GetSamplingMode` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::DESIRED_WIDTH` | `SetDesiredWidth`, `GetDesiredWidth` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::DESIRED_HEIGHT` | `SetDesiredHeight`, `GetDesiredHeight` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::LOAD_POLICY` | `SetLoadPolicy`, `GetLoadPolicy` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::RELEASE_POLICY` | `SetReleasePolicy`, `GetReleasePolicy` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::SYNCHRONOUS_LOADING` | `SetSynchronousLoading`, `IsSynchronousLoading` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::IMAGE_LOAD_WITH_VIEW_SIZE` | `SetImageLoadWithViewSize`, `IsImageLoadWithViewSizeEnabled` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::ALPHA_MASK_URL` | `SetAlphaMaskUrl`, `GetAlphaMaskUrl` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::CROP_TO_MASK` | `SetCropToMask`, `IsCropToMask` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::MASKING_MODE` | `SetMaskingMode`, `GetMaskingMode` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::PLACEHOLDER_IMAGE` | `SetPlaceholderUrl`, `GetPlaceholderUrl` |
| `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::PIXEL_AREA` | `SetPixelArea`, `GetPixelArea` |

`Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageViewPropertyIndex` is the index definition shared by `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property` and the integration implementation. For normal dali-ui app code, use `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::ALPHA_MASK_URL` and `Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::Property::BATCH_SIZE` through the `AnimatedImageView::Property` owner rather than depending directly on the shared index definition.

Loading policy can be configured with typed setters:

```cpp
Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView image = Dali::Ui::AnimatedImageView::New("images/card.gif");

image
  .SetLoadPolicy(Dali::Ui::Image::LoadPolicy::IMMEDIATE)
  .SetReleasePolicy(Dali::Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy::DESTROYED)
  .SetSynchronousLoading(false);

Dali::Ui::Image::LoadPolicy loadPolicy = image.GetLoadPolicy();
Dali::Ui::Image::ReleasePolicy releasePolicy = image.GetReleasePolicy();
const bool synchronous = image.IsSynchronousLoading();

(void)loadPolicy;
(void)releasePolicy;
(void)synchronous;
```
